For marketing partners

Everything you need to make this look like SpotsNow.

The logo, the colors, the type, the voice. The patterns we use on the site, in the deck, on the posters. Read this once. Build with it forever.

01 / Mission
What we're building

The next 2 million brands haven't done podcast advertising yet.

Most of them never will, the way it works today. Cold emails, six-week negotiations, promo codes pretending to be measurement. We're building the platform that brings them in, and the intelligence layer that defines what advertising looks like in an AI-native world.

A category we're building, not joining.
$32B
Inventory in long-form media advertising globally
50%
Of podcast inventory goes unsold every week
4.2x
Average ROAS on host-read podcast advertising
02 / Product
The product, in one line

The one stop platform for long-form media advertising.

Discovery, negotiation, creative, attribution, optimization. One platform. Five minutes to launch. Twelve hours to live. The whole thing used to take six weeks.

Without SpotsNow

Manual, opaque, pre-internet.

  • Cold email shows or chase agencies
  • 4 to 6 weeks to negotiate one campaign
  • Promo codes guess at attribution
  • No real-time view of who's running or how it's performing
With SpotsNow

Self-serve, transparent, live in 12 hours.

  • Discover and book in one platform. No agency, no calls.
  • Set up a campaign in 5 minutes. Live in 12 hours.
  • Audio pixel attribution. 7x more accurate than promo codes.
  • Real-time dashboard for spend, plays, conversions, ROAS
03 / Logo
The mark, three ways

Three lockups, three colorways.

Horizontal is the default. Use the icon only when SpotsNow has already been named on the same surface. Don't remake the mark, don't redraw it, don't outline it. The files are right here.

Horizontal
Stacked
Icon
Primary
On light surfaces
Brand
Coral accent
Reverse
On coral or dark
04 / Usage
Clear space and don'ts

Give the mark room to breathe.

Clear space is non-negotiable. When in doubt, add more. The mark earns its impact when nothing crowds it.

Clear space

Half the x-height on every side.

The dashed line shows the minimum padding. No other element, no other logo, no other type inside it.

0.5x

Minimum size: 24px wide for the icon, 96px wide for the horizontal lockup.

Don't

Four ways to break the brand.

Seen in the wild, all of these. Don't do them.

Don't recolor the mark
Salmon, black, or white only. Ever.
Don't stretch or squash
Always scale proportionally.
Don't add effects
No shadows, glows, or outlines.
Don't place on busy photos
Use a solid color overlay instead.
05 / Color
The palette

Five colors. Salmon does the work.

Salmon does the work. Black is for type. Navy is the heavy contrast surface for stat sections and trust moments. Mint is the system signal: category tags, positive metrics, value pills. White is for breathing room. Don't add a sixth without asking.

06 / Type
The voice on the page

Inter for everything. Parkinsans for the wordmark.

Inter for everything. Four weights, used in four roles. Body is one size, one color, one weight, everywhere. The site uses Inter, the deck uses Inter, the posters use Inter. Parkinsans only ever lives inside the logo file.

PRIMARY
Aa
Inter
fonts.google.com/specimen/Inter
ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ
abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz
0123456789
WORDMARK ONLY
Aa
Parkinsans
fonts.google.com/specimen/Parkinsans
USED IN THE LOGO FILE ONLY
NOT FOR SETTING UI OR CONTENT TYPE
SEMIBOLD WEIGHT IN THE LOCKUP
DISPLAY
Inter ExtraBold 800 · 40-68px · -3% tracking · 1.04 line height
Live in 12 hours. Set up in 5.
SUBHEAD
Inter ExtraBold 800 · 22-26px · -2.5% tracking
Self-serve podcast advertising for the next 2 million brands.
BODY
Inter Regular 400 · 16px · 1.55 line height · -0.5% tracking
Brands list a campaign in five minutes. Hosts approve in one click. Attribution comes from the audio pixel, not a promo code, so you finally know what's working.
EYEBROW / KICKER
Inter Bold 700 · 13px · ALL CAPS · +6% tracking
For Advertisers
Body consistency rule
Body text is always Inter Regular 400, 16px, 1.55 line height, color #2B2B2B. Use this for every paragraph in every context. Never set body in a different size, weight, or color "for variety."
07 / Buttons
CTAs and actions

Three buttons. One is the hero.

Salmon for every conversion moment. The salmon button is where a user gives us their attention or their money. Ink is for secondary, outline is for tertiary or in-card. That's the whole rule.

PRIMARY
bg #F06060 · text #FFF
14px 28px · 10px radius
Inter Semibold 600
SECONDARY
bg #000 · text #FFF
14px 28px · 10px radius
Inter Semibold 600
TERTIARY
bg #FFF · border #E8E8E8
14px 28px · 10px radius
Inter Semibold 600
08 / Marketing layouts
Two reusable patterns

Posters and product showcases. Two layouts that travel.

When you need to make something salmon-shaped (a social post, a banner, a landing block) reach for one of these. They're the canonical SpotsNow marketing units. Don't invent new ones unless you have a reason.

SALMON POSTER
Big white headline. Product card peeking in.
Salmon background, white Inter ExtraBold headline taking up the top two-thirds, and a real product card mockup floating in from the bottom. Use it for social, ads, hero banners. The headline should be the offer or the punchline. Don't put a logo on top. The card has the icon already.
Background
#F06060
Headline
Inter 800, white
Card
White, 16px radius, real UI
Logo placement
Bottom right, white icon
PRODUCT SHOWCASE
Tilted UI cards. Soft gray surface.
Three or four real product cards arranged at slight angles on a #F7F8FA background. Use it on the deck, the website, or anywhere you need to show "this is what the product actually looks like." Real screens beat illustrated mockups every time.
Background
#F7F8FA
Card rotation
2-6 degrees, varied
Shadow
Soft, 24-40px blur, 4% opacity
Source
Real UI screenshots only

10 / Voice
How we talk

Specific, fast, founder-casual.

We sound like a founder writing a Slack message to a smart friend. Not a brand running an ad campaign. Be specific. Use real numbers. Have an opinion. Skip filler. Ban the em dash. If a sentence could appear on any SaaS landing page, rewrite it until it can't.

✓DO
"We just shipped audio pixel attribution."
Why. Specific, present tense, no hedging.
"Set up a campaign in 5 minutes. Live in 12 hours."
Why. Concrete numbers beat adjectives.
"Podcast advertising used to take 4 weeks. We made it take an afternoon."
Why. Founder voice. Before and after.
"Hot take: promo codes were never measurement."
Why. Opinion is fine. Generic posturing isn't.
✕DON'T
"Excited to announce we are revolutionizing podcast advertising."
Why not. Corporate filler. "Excited to announce" is banned.
"Empower brands to unlock the power of audio at scale."
Why not. Empower, unlock, leverage: all banned.
"We help — we really do — connect brands with hosts."
Why not. Em dashes are banned. Hyphens or full stops only.
"Touching base on our innovative new platform."
Why not. Touching base, innovative, platform-as-buzzword: all banned.
11 / Legal
What we can publish

Green light, red light.

We sit on a lot of competitive and creator data. Most of it is fair game in marketing content. Some of it isn't, and the wrong post can land us in a lawyer's inbox. Read this before you publish anything that names names or quotes numbers. When in doubt, ask Cam.

GREEN LIGHT · SHIP IT
Aggregate industry spend data
Total quarterly spend by category, year-over-year trends, top 10 categories. Public, useful, on-brand.
SpotsNow customer case studies
TEQSLA at 3.5x ROAS, named partner shows hitting 5x. Use only with the brand's written approval.
Founder story and operational reality
The 4-week first campaign, cold-call chaos, why we built the platform. This is core content.
Public ad creative analysis
Anything that aired on a public podcast feed can be quoted, transcribed, and dissected.
RED LIGHT · ASK FIRST
Individual creator earnings
Never publish how much a specific show earns from any advertiser. Legal risk, even if the data is in our system.
Named brand spend without permission
AG1, Macy's, Airbnb monthly spend numbers stay internal. We can talk about "a major DTC brand," not the brand by name.
Show ranking lists by revenue
"Top 50 most lucrative podcasts" reads as authoritative and exposes us if a single number is wrong or unauthorized.
Implied performance claims
Don't promise ROAS multiples without a specific, attributable case study and the customer's written sign-off.

Get the assets, ask a question, ship the work.
